rationale: The citing paper uses Irmak, Block, and Fitzsimons (2005) in specific argumentative roles, not just in a generic literature list. In Study 2, it explicitly contrasts its design with Irmak et al.'s energy-drink placebo setup to argue that participants here are told they are consuming plain water, helping rule out a placebo-based explanation. In the General Discussion, it again uses Irmak et al. to interpret individual differences in brand effects (motivation-sensitive placebo responses) and position its own moderator and mechanism claims relative to that stream.
relation_description: The citing paper substantively positions its contribution against the placebo-effect stream represented by Irmak et al. (2005), using that study as a contrastive benchmark for mechanism and design. It leverages Irmak et al.'s findings to frame boundary conditions and interpret how its self-efficacy account differs from placebo-based brand effects.
